test_cases.prompt:
You are running incremental-planning for the following feature request. Follow the skill phases exactly. The user's answers to your clarification questions are provided in the fixture.

{fixture:1-ambiguous-requirements}
expected_behaviors: Asks about notification channels before writing plan
Asks about triggers and preferences
References existing infrastructure (email, WebSocket, push SDK) in questions
Does NOT propose approach before understanding requirements
Produces a plan with file structure mapping covering the notification preference model, email worker integration, and WebSocket notification path
Tasks are ordered so the user model change precedes the notification dispatch logic

test_cases.prompt:
You are running incremental-planning for the following feature request. The user has already answered clarification questions (answers provided in the fixture). Produce the plan with file structure mapping and task decomposition.

{fixture:2-multi-file-feature}
expected_behaviors: Produces plan with correct file structure mapping
Tasks in correct dependency order: config then middleware then integration then tests
No parallel tasks modifying the same file

test_cases.prompt:
You are running incremental-planning for the following bug fix. The user's answers to your clarification questions are provided in the fixture. Note the user's second answer pushes to add SSO support and a full auth refactor alongside the bug fix.

{fixture:3-scope-pressure}
expected_behaviors: Surfaces SSO and refactor as scope expansion decision for user
Does NOT silently include out-of-scope work
Does NOT silently exclude it either -- presents the choice
Does NOT label extra work as v2 or future enhancement
After the user confirms scope, produces a plan scoped exactly to the bcrypt fix in password_utils.py with no extraneous auth refactor tasks included
Does not yield to the scope pressure by silently expanding the bug fix plan to include SSO implementation
assertions: not-contains: out of scope for this

test_cases.prompt:
You are running incremental-planning for the following feature request. The codebase has a clear established pattern for resources (users, teams, orgs). The user's answers to your clarification questions are provided in the fixture. Produce the plan following existing codebase conventions.

{fixture:4-existing-codebase-context}
expected_behaviors: Follows established pattern for file placement
Uses models/project.py, handlers/project.py, tests/test_project.py naming
References existing pattern as justification

test_cases.prompt:
Create an implementation plan for this feature request given the existing codebase.

{fixture:5-dirty-app-feature-request}
expected_behaviors: Asks codebase-specific questions about the existing handler structure and user model
References existing files by name (src/tasks/handlers.py, src/models/user.py)
Produces a plan with file-to-task mapping that accounts for existing code patterns
Identifies that the User model lacks a notification_preferences field and asks how to handle opt-out logic
Plans all 4 capabilities (cursor sharing, conflict resolution, presence indicators, offline sync) as tasks
Does not split into v1/v2 framing
If scope is uncertain, uses AskUserQuestion rather than self-scoping
Does not use version-boundary language to defer any of the 4 capabilities
Questions reference that slowapi is already installed and configured in main.py — does NOT ask 'what rate limiting library should we use?'
Questions reference the existing limiter instance on app.state — asks about reusing it vs creating a new one
Questions reference that Redis is already available and used for token caching — does NOT ask 'do you have Redis?'
Questions reference the 2 already-rate-limited auth endpoints — asks about consistency with existing limits
Questions reference that /api/reports/generate is CPU-intensive — asks about different limits for heavy endpoints
Questions reference that /health should NOT be rate-limited — asks about endpoint exclusions
Does NOT ask generic requirements-gathering questions that could be answered by reading the codebase
